description_long: This is a large, four-engine military surveillance airplane with a distinctive circular radar dome mounted above the fuselage, characteristic of airborne early warning and control (AEW&C) aircraft. The fuselage is robust and streamlined, with a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit featuring multiple windows. The wings are high-mounted and swept back, each supporting two turbofan engines beneath. The tail section features a T-tail configuration with a prominent horizontal stabilizer mounted atop a single vertical fin. The aircraft is painted in a light gray color with red star insignias on the tail and wings, and has visible landing gear doors indicating retractable landing gear.
description: Large military surveillance airplane with a circular radar dome, high swept wings, four underwing engines, and a T-tail. Painted light gray with red star insignias.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular radar dome above the fuselage, four underwing engines, and the T-tail.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the high-mounted swept wings, radar dome, cockpit windows, and red star insignia on the tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the T-tail, horizontal stabilizer, and the full span of the swept wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the radar dome, engine placement, and red star insignia on the tail and wing.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, swept-back wings with no visible winglets and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four turbofan engines mounted under the wings, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear unit.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
